identificationModule.briefTitle: High Intensity Interval Training in Chronic Heart Failure
identificationModule.officialTitle: The Effect of High-intensity Interval Training and Moderate Intensity Continuous Training on Cardiopulmonary Functions in Chronic Heart Failure

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The aim of this study is to investigate the effect of high-intensity interval training and moderate intensity continuous training on cardiopulmonary functions in chronic heart failure.
descriptionModule.detailedDescription:
enroll chronic heart failure patients into 3 randomized controlled group

* high intensity interval training
* moderate intensity continous training
* control

perform cardica rehab for 12 weeks, compare primary and secondary endpoints between baseline and 12 weeks results

armGroups.label: High intensity interval training
interventionNames: Behavioral: Cardiac rehabilitation comprised aerobic exercise on a treadmill accompanied by an electrocardiogram monitoring device and a blood pressure monitor

armGroups.label: Moderate intensity continous training
interventionNames: Behavioral: Cardiac rehabilitation comprised aerobic exercise on a treadmill accompanied by an electrocardiogram monitoring device and a blood pressure monitor

armGroups.label: Control
interventionNames: Behavioral: Cardiac rehabilitation comprised aerobic exercise on a treadmill accompanied by an electrocardiogram monitoring device and a blood pressure monitor

interventions.name: Cardiac rehabilitation comprised aerobic exercise on a treadmill accompanied by an electrocardiogram monitoring device and a blood pressure monitor
interventions.description: aerobic exercise on a treadmill (T7M, Motus, Paju, South Korea) accompanied by an electrocardiogram monitoring device (Q-Tel Rehabilitation Management System, Mortara Inc., Milwaukee, WI, USA) and a blood pressure monitor

primaryOutcomes.measure: Maximal or peak oxygen consumption (VO2max or VO2peak, same meaning) (kg/ml/min)
primaryOutcomes.description: at the end of training, from baseline to 12 weeks
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data

secondaryOutcomes.description: The anaerobic threshold is the highest exercise intensity that you can sustain for a prolonged period without lactate substantially building up in your blood.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data
compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data
compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data
compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data
compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data
compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data
compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data

secondaryOutcomes.measure: depression scale by Patient Health Questionnaire
secondaryOutcomes.description: Patient Health Questionnaire (PHQ; range, 0-27; higher score = severe depression; cut-off score of 5 for minimal depression)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data

secondaryOutcomes.measure: anxiety scale by Generalized Anxiety Disorder Scale
secondaryOutcomes.description: Generalized Anxiety Disorder Scale (GAD; range, 0-21; higher score = severe anxiety; cut-off score of 5 for mild anxiety)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data

secondaryOutcomes.measure: fatigue scale by fatigue severity scale
secondaryOutcomes.description: fatigue severity scale (FSS; range, 9-63; higher score = severe fatigue; cut-off score of 4 for fatigue)
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data

secondaryOutcomes.description: MacNew QoL questionnaire is a measurement tool for patients with cardiac disease that measures physical, emotional, and social functional levels to provide overall QoL.
secondaryOutcomes.timeFrame: compare baseline data with 12 weeks rehabilitation data

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* medically stable chronic heart failure
* NYHA II-III
* can perform cardiopulmonary exercise test
* after 3months with proper medication
* age 30 to 70
* ejection fraction\< 40%

Exclusion Criteria:

* contraindication to cardiopulmonary exercise test
* musculoskeletal problem who cannot perform cardiopulmonary exercise test
* cognition deficit who cannot understand directions
* severe medial illness

overallOfficials.name: Kyung-Lim Joa, M.D.
overallOfficials.affiliation: Department of physical medicine and rehabilitation, Inha University hospital
